965d9d27.title: AWS Solution Architect
965d9d27.description:
This is an excellent opportunity to be one of the key people involved in governing digital and marketing platforms and roadmap across the organisation. As part of the Corporate Technology Architecture team, your role is to guide, advise, and recommend how our digital landscape evolves over the coming years and support the delivery teams offering outstanding solutions.
   

  

 Role Overview:
   

  

 We are looking for a seasoned AWS Solution Architect with over 10 years of experience in designing and delivering robust, scalable, and secure cloud solutions. The ideal candidate should have deep technical expertise across a broad range of AWS services, with a strong focus on data integration, serverless computing, enterprise search, and secure web hosting. This role is critical in shaping our cloud architecture strategy and ensuring alignment with business goals.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities:
   

  

* Solution Architecture and implement solutions using AWS Glue, Lambda, API Gateway, and Kafka for both real\-time and batch data processing.
* Design and deploy enterprise search platforms such as Elasticsearch to support scalable and efficient data discovery.
* Lead the development and hosting of web applications on AWS, ensuring high availability, performance, and cost\-efficiency.
* Define and implement security architectures, including WAF (Web Application Firewall) policies and CDN caching strategies (e.g., CloudFront).
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure cloud solutions meet security, compliance, and operational requirements.
* Conduct architecture reviews, performance tuning, and cost optimisation exercises.
* Document architectural decisions, patterns, and best practices.
* Work with Business Stakeholders, Product Owners, Business Analysts to understand functional and non\-functional requirements and interact with other cross\-functional teams.
* Contribute to Customer’s digital marketing strategies based on industry best practices.
* Help project team to size \& estimate project implementation and identify resources and skillset needed for delivery of solution.


 Required Skills \& Experience:
   

  

* 10\+ years of experience in cloud architecture, with a strong focus on AWS.
* Hands\-on expertise with AWS Glue, Lambda, API Gateway, Kafka.
* Experience with Elasticsearch or similar enterprise search technologies.
* Proficiency in CloudFront, WAF, and other AWS security services.
* Experience hosting and scaling websites using AWS (e.g., S3, EC2, ECS, Amplify).
* Strong understanding of cloud security, IAM, and compliance frameworks.
* Experience with CI/CD pipelines and Infrastructure as Code (e.g., CloudFormation, Terraform).
* Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.


 Preferred Qualifications:
   

  

* AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Professional (preferred but not mandatory).
* Experience in enterprise\-scale environments or regulated industries.
* Familiarity with observability tools (e.g., CloudWatch, Datadog).


**Personal Skills And Capabilities**
* Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to communicate technical information to non\-technical partners and to listen to identified concerns and adapt accordingly.
* Ability to influence across multiple functions (technical and non\-technical)
* Quick Leader: Should understand the new implementation patterns and adapt.
* A continuous desire and willingness to learn and develop your current knowledge and abilities.
* Flexible in approach, adapting plans and strategies to help handle risks around ambiguity.
* Problem solver with a strong intuition for business and proficient in current technological trends and business concepts


**Benefits**
 We are looking for intellectually curious people, passionate about the bigger picture of how technology industry is evolving, ready to ask difficult questions and deal with complicated scenarios! If you are creative and a problem solver, this is the place to be as will be supporting you to fast\-forward your career!

f7fde335.title: Solutions Architect -AWS
f7fde335.description:
**About Us**
 At Rhythm, our values form the foundation of our business. We are passionate about customer success, innovation, and our employees. They guide our actions, decisions, and interactions, ensuring that we consistently make a positive impact on the world around us.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Position Overview
   

  

 We are seeking a rare, highly skilled hybrid AWS Solutions Architect \& Software Developer to join our core product engineering team. In this role, you will bridge the gap between high\-level cloud architecture and hands\-on backend development. You will be responsible for designing, implementing, and code\-authoring the robust cloud infrastructure that powers our data core.
   

  

 The primary focus of this position is architecting and developing the high\-throughput pipelines that process raw "fleet exhaust data" (noise) in AWS and funnel actionable summaries (signals) into our enterprise presentation layer. You will heavily focus on complex data orchestrations and integrations across AWS, Salesforce Data Cloud, MuleSoft, and critical third\-party fleet compliance ecosystems.
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

 As a hybrid Architect and Developer, your responsibilities will span architectural design, core systems programming, and cross\-platform ecosystem integration:
   

  

 Cloud Architecture \& Strategy
   

  

* Design \& Scale: Architect and optimize secure, scalable, and cost\-effective cloud infrastructures capable of ingesting massive, high\-velocity streaming data environments.
* Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Develop and maintain production\-ready CloudFormation or Terraform templates to ensure fully automated, repeatable environment deployments.
* CI/CD Leadership: Establish and govern standardized CI/CD pipelines that continuously decrease time\-to\-production for new backend features while enforcing rigorous quality and testing gates.
* Security \& Trust: Design and implement a robust Cloud Trust Architecture ensuring compliance with stringent data protection best practices and role\-based access control.


 Backend Software Engineering
   

  

* Write Robust Backend Code: Write clean, highly performant, and scalable code (primarily utilizing Python or Node.js) to build core AWS backend services, APIs, and microservices.
* Data Processing \& AI: Program state\-of\-the\-art serverless workflows utilizing AWS Lambda, EventBridge, and Amazon Bedrock to compute derived risk indicators (e.g., speeding minutes per 100 miles, trend slope tracking).
* Database \& Knowledge Lake Management: Optimize structured and unstructured data persistence strategies across Amazon S3, RDS instances, and managed Vector Databases.
* Testing \& Quality Assurance: Author and run extensive unit and integration tests for AWS\-based applications, lead code reviews, and aggressively improve core data processing algorithms.


 Multi\-Platform Ecosystem Integration
   

  

* Ecosystem Orchestration: Architect the ingestion paths for high\-velocity telematics and IoT data, isolating massive volume processing in AWS before translating relevant indicators to executive CRM workflows.
* Telematics \& Compliance Integration: Build and maintain direct API data pipelines integrating heavy enterprise platforms and third\-party systems including:
+ Telematics Infrastructure: Deep integration with providers like Verizon Connect (VZC) to ingest real\-time engine diagnostics, GPS, and harsh event data.
+ Regulatory Compliance: Integrations with FMCSA API systems (including SAFER, SMS, A\&I, and DataQs datasets).
+ Risk \& Personnel Platforms: Synchronized identity management pipelines with Motor Vehicle Record (MVR) monitoring and Learning Management Systems (LMS) such as Samba Safety, Sentix, or related Risk Cloud environments.

* Salesforce \& MuleSoft Collaboration: Partner closely with application teams to orchestrate end\-to\-end event\-driven flows using MuleSoft for bidirectional Roster CRUD logic, linking the AWS Knowledge Lake cleanly with Salesforce Data Cloud and AgentForce.


**Requirements**
 What We're Looking For
   

  

* Education: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
* Experience:
+ Minimum of 5–7 years of proven hands\-on software development experience writing clean production code.
+ Minimum of 2 years of dedicated experience acting as an AWS Solutions Architect designing complex, multi\-system integration topographies.

* AWS Certifications: Valid AWS Certifications are strongly preferred (e.g., AWS Certified Solutions Architect – Professional, AWS Certified Developer – Associate).
* Technical Proficiency:
+ Languages: Advanced proficiency in Python or Node.js.
+ AWS Infrastructure: Deep knowledge of AWS S3, Lambda, EventBridge, Amazon Bedrock, Lex, Connect, SES, and native database layers (RDS, DynamoDB, or Vector DBs).
+ Integration Tech: Exceptional familiarity with building and consuming web services across API, REST, and RPC architectures.
+ Containers: Experience working with Docker and Kubernetes environments for containerized compute deployment.

* Domain Context: Prior experience writing applications that parse telematics data, cellular/IoT streams, geographic spatial boundaries, or government/compliance API interfaces is a significant advantage.
* Soft Skills: Exceptional analytical and problem\-solving skills, with a dedicated approach to data\-driven technical execution and clear, transparent team communication.

5655d510.title: Chief Platform & Cloud Architect (AWS | Platform Engineering | AI Operations)
5655d510.description:
**Chief Platform \& Cloud Architect (AWS \| Platform Engineering \| AI Operations)** 

**📍 Location:** 

 Remote (India)
 




**💼 Employment Type:** 

 Full\-Time
 




**🌍 Work Mode:** 

 100% Remote
 




**💰 Experience:** 

 12\+ Years
 




  




**About the Role** 


 We are looking for a
 
**Chief Platform \& Cloud Architect** 

 to define and lead the cloud platform that powers a growing portfolio of enterprise SaaS products.
 





 This is not a traditional Cloud Architect role focused on a single application. You will be responsible for building and governing the cloud foundation across multiple products, engineering teams, and environments.
 





 You will own AWS platform architecture, Landing Zones, cloud governance, platform engineering, security, observability, reliability engineering, FinOps, and AI\-powered cloud operations.
 





 The ideal candidate has built enterprise\-scale cloud platforms, established Platform Engineering practices, implemented Infrastructure as Code, and introduced AI\-driven operational automation that improves reliability, developer productivity, security, and cloud efficiency.
 





 If you enjoy solving large\-scale platform engineering challenges and want to shape the future of intelligent cloud operations, this role offers a unique opportunity to make a lasting impact.
 




  




**What You'll Do** 
**Cloud Platform Strategy \& Architecture** 
* Define and own the enterprise AWS cloud strategy across multiple SaaS products.
* Design and govern AWS Landing Zones using AWS Organizations, Organizational Units (OUs), AWS Control Tower, and Service Control Policies (SCPs).
* Build secure, scalable, multi\-account cloud environments for Development, QA, Staging, Production, and Internal Engineering.
* Define enterprise\-wide cloud governance, architecture standards, and engineering best practices.
* Design highly available, fault\-tolerant, and disaster\-resilient cloud platforms capable of operating at organizational scale.
* Standardize Infrastructure as Code using Terraform and AWS CDK to ensure repeatable, secure, and version\-controlled infrastructure deployments.


**Platform Engineering** 
* Build and evolve an enterprise Platform Engineering capability.
* Design and implement Internal Developer Platforms (IDP), Golden Paths, and self\-service infrastructure.
* Improve Developer Experience (DevEx) through reusable platform services and automation.
* Build standardized CI/CD pipelines and cloud provisioning frameworks.
* Define reusable cloud architecture patterns that accelerate engineering teams while maintaining governance.


**Cloud Infrastructure \& Networking** 


 Architect and optimize enterprise cloud infrastructure across services including:
 



* Amazon EC2
* Amazon EKS
* AWS Lambda
* Amazon RDS
* Amazon S3
* CloudFront
* Route 53
* VPC
* Transit Gateway
* IAM
* Secrets Manager
* AWS KMS
* CloudWatch
* EventBridge




 while ensuring scalability, resilience, security, and operational excellence.
 



**Security \& Cloud Governance** 
* Define enterprise security architecture across cloud infrastructure, applications, and data.
* Implement IAM least\-privilege access models and Zero Trust principles.
* Manage encryption, secrets management, and secure service\-to\-service communication.
* Establish continuous compliance through AWS Config, CloudTrail, Security Hub, GuardDuty, and automated policy enforcement.
* Drive governance standards across all AWS accounts and environments.


**Reliability Engineering \& Observability** 


 Own the organization's cloud reliability strategy by implementing:
 



* Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) practices
* Service Level Indicators (SLIs)
* Service Level Objectives (SLOs)
* Error Budgets
* Production Readiness Reviews
* Operational Runbooks
* Disaster Recovery Planning
* High Availability Architecture




 Build and operate a modern observability platform using:
 



* Prometheus
* Grafana
* OpenTelemetry
* CloudWatch
* Distributed Tracing
* Centralized Logging
* Alerting
* Performance Dashboards




 Drive continuous improvements in platform reliability, availability, and operational efficiency.
 



**FinOps \& Cloud Cost Optimization** 
* Lead enterprise cloud cost optimization initiatives.
* Implement tagging standards and cloud governance policies.
* Optimize infrastructure through rightsizing, Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and capacity planning.
* Build visibility into cloud utilization and operational efficiency.
* Partner with engineering teams to improve cloud economics without compromising reliability.


**AI\-Powered Cloud Operations** 


 Build the next generation of intelligent cloud operations by introducing:
 



* Agentic AI
* AIOps
* AI\-assisted Infrastructure Automation
* AWS Bedrock
* Intelligent Incident Detection
* Automated Root Cause Analysis
* Predictive Scaling
* AI\-powered Operational Runbooks
* Cost Anomaly Detection
* Self\-healing Infrastructure Workflows




 Transform cloud operations from reactive incident management to proactive, intelligent automation.
 




  




**What We're Looking For** 
**Experience** 
* 12\+ years of experience in Cloud Engineering, Platform Engineering, DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), or Cloud Infrastructure.
* Proven experience designing and operating enterprise\-scale AWS platforms.
* Hands\-on experience implementing AWS Landing Zones, AWS Organizations, AWS Control Tower, and Service Control Policies (SCPs).
* Strong background building Platform Engineering capabilities and Internal Developer Platforms (IDP).
* Experience managing cloud environments across Development, QA, Staging, and Production.
* Deep understanding of enterprise cloud security, governance, networking, and compliance.
* Strong experience building enterprise observability platforms.
* Experience driving cloud modernization and infrastructure transformation initiatives.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
* Strong hands\-on technical capability with architecture, automation, and cloud engineering.





 ﻿
 




**Technical Expertise** 
**AWS** 


 AWS Organizations • AWS Control Tower • Landing Zones • Service Control Policies (SCPs) • EC2 • EKS • Lambda • IAM • VPC • Transit Gateway • Route 53 • CloudFront • RDS • S3 • CloudWatch • EventBridge • Secrets Manager • AWS KMS
 



**Infrastructure \& Automation** 


 Terraform • AWS CDK • CloudFormation • Docker • Kubernetes • Helm • GitOps • ArgoCD • GitHub Actions • Jenkins • Python • Bash
 



**Observability** 


 Prometheus • Grafana • OpenTelemetry • CloudWatch • Datadog • Splunk • ELK • New Relic
 



**Platform Engineering** 


 Internal Developer Platforms (IDP) • Golden Paths • Developer Experience (DevEx) • Self\-Service Infrastructure • Infrastructure as Code • Cloud Governance • Platform Engineering
 



**AI \& Intelligent Operations** 


 Agentic AI • AIOps • AWS Bedrock • AI\-assisted Operations • Intelligent Automation • LLM\-powered Infrastructure Automation • MCP (Model Context Protocol) • AI\-driven Incident Response
 




  




**Preferred Qualifications** 
* AWS Solutions Architect – Professional
* AWS DevOps Engineer – Professional
* AWS Security Specialty
* AWS Advanced Networking Specialty
* Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA)
* TOGAF Certification
* Experience in enterprise SaaS or product engineering organizations.
* Experience building cloud platforms supporting multiple products and engineering teams.
* Exposure to AI\-powered operational tooling and cloud automation.



  




**Ideal Background** 


 Candidates with experience in roles such as:
 



* Chief Cloud Architect
* Principal Cloud Architect
* Principal Platform Engineer
* Platform Engineering Director
* Cloud Platform Architect
* Director of Platform Engineering
* Head of Cloud Engineering
* Principal Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)
* Engineering Leader – Cloud \& DevOps
* Enterprise Cloud Architect
* AWS Platform Architect
* Infrastructure Architecture Leader



  




**Why Join Us?** 


 You'll have the opportunity to architect and govern the cloud platform that powers an entire organization—not just a single product.
 





 This role offers the chance to shape enterprise cloud strategy, build modern Platform Engineering capabilities, introduce AI\-powered cloud operations, and establish the standards that enable engineering teams to deliver secure, scalable, and highly reliable software at scale.
 





 If you're passionate about Platform Engineering, cloud architecture, intelligent automation, and building world\-class cloud foundations, we'd love to hear from you.

211ac423.title: Manager - Business Architecture
211ac423.description:
**About the Role** 




  





 As WrkTalk scales its enterprise deployments, we are building a Business Architecture function that sits at the intersection of technology and client delivery. This role owns the end\-to\-end deployment of WrkTalk instances for enterprise clients — from internal coordination through go\-live — freeing the sales team to focus on client relationships and revenue.
 



  





**What You'll Own** 



* Take complete ownership of every client deployment from sales handover to go\-live
* Liaise internally across Backend, Frontend, DevOps, Testing teams to deliver each instance on time and correctly
* Manage all pre\-deployment client queries on infrastructure requirements, coordinating with DevOps and Backend to resolve them
* Drive installation calls — including screen shares, environment access sessions, IP whitelisting, SMTP configuration, and networking handshakes
* Troubleshoot post\-deployment issues (traffic routing, call connectivity, infosec clearances) by pulling in the right internal resources
* Maintain rigorous deployment checklists and sign off on every instance before client handover
* Coordinate internal attendance for client\-facing calls — identifying who needs to be on a call and securing their availability
* Act as the first line of defence for product integrity — when sales brings client customisation requests, you evaluate them against product architecture and articulate clearly and logically why WrkTalk is a single\-product platform, not a bespoke solution, ensuring client expectations are set correctly from day one
* Serve as the single point of internal coordination so the sales team's only touchpoint is you



  





**Who You Are** 



* B.Tech / B.E. from a reputed institution (IIT preferred) with 2–3 years of experience in a technology\-intensive environment
* Not necessarily a programmer, but deeply fluent in how technology systems work — infra, networking, application layers
* Strong product mindset — you instinctively think in terms of scalable, standardised solutions and are comfortable holding the line on product boundaries without being dismissive of client needs
* Organised to a fault — you work from checklists, track every open item, and nothing falls through the cracks
* A natural coordinator who can hold multiple concurrent deployments without losing thread
* Comfortable on client calls — confident, articulate, and able to translate technical complexity into plain language
* Ambitious: this role is deliberately designed as a techno\-commercial springboard for someone eyeing a top MBA programme



  





**Why This Role** 




 This is not a support function. You will have direct client exposure, own high\-stakes deployments for marquee enterprises, and develop a rare combination of technical depth and business acumen. For an engineer looking to build a compelling MBA application — or simply to grow fast in a scaling B2B product company — this is an unusually rich two\-year stint.
 



  





**Location:** 
 Navi Mumbai
 



**Reporting to:** 
 Founders / Senior Leadership

dceefd45.title: Infrastructure Delivery Manager, DC Operations, AWS Infra Operations
dceefd45.description:
**Description**
 AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.
   

  

 You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.
   

  

 We are looking for a senior, strategic Rack Lifecycle Cluster Manager to join our Infrastructure Operations team in BOM, owning the end\-to\-end rack lifecycle across three clusters. This is a high\-impact leadership role responsible for the full continuum of rack operations — from demand forecasting and rack landing planning, through Late Binding cabling, rack installation and handoff, to end\-of\-life decommissioning.
   

  

 You will lead a central, cross\-functional team of managers and technicians, setting the strategic direction for rack lifecycle operations while driving operational excellence, process standardization, and automation at scale. You will be the single accountable owner for ensuring every rack needed for customer capacity lands on time, is cabled and installed to standard, and is decommissioned with zero compliance gaps — across all three clusters under your purview.
   

  

 Your work will directly influence AWS's ability to deliver infrastructure capacity to customers reliably and at the lowest possible cost, while maintaining the highest standards of safety, security, and environmental compliance.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* Own the multi\-cluster rack lifecycle strategy across BOM, aligning delivery roadmaps with AWS capacity plans, finance targets, and regional infrastructure goals
* Serve as the senior escalation point and decision\-maker across all rack lifecycle phases — deliveries, cabling, installation, handoff, and decommissioning — for three clusters simultaneously
* Define and drive long\-range operational plans, including workforce planning, tooling investments, and process transformation initiatives across the cluster portfolio
* Partner with senior stakeholders across Capacity Planning, Network Engineering, DCEO, Supply Chain, and AWS Leadership to align priorities and resolve cross\-functional dependencies
* Own end\-to\-end inbound capacity delivery from demand forecast through physical rack landing across all three clusters, ensuring on\-time delivery against committed customer capacity dates
* Drive rack landing planning in coordination with Late Binding, PIC, and facilities teams — validating space, power, cooling, and fiber readiness ahead of each deployment wave
* Provide strategic oversight for Late Binding cabling operations, ensuring fiber/copper cabling, patching schedules, OTDR/power testing, and labeling standards are executed to specification
* Oversee rack installation operations across all three clusters, ensuring site readiness, rack placement, power and cooling sign\-off, and network device installation are completed per standards
* Ensure clean, documented handoffs to Data Center Operations teams, including updated CMDB/DCIM records, port maps, test results, and completed workflow sign\-offs
* Own the decommissioning pipeline for end\-of\-life racks, coordinating with Capacity Planning, Network Operations, DCLM/ALM, and MARS teams to schedule graceful shutdowns with zero service impact
* Ensure full EHS and MOP compliance across all physical decommission activities
* Define, track, and report on cluster\-level KPIs including racks landed vs. plan, Late Binding cabling completion %, capacity ready dates, and decom backlog vs. flightpath goal
* Lead WBR and Kingpin reporting for the BOM cluster portfolio
* Lead, mentor, and develop a multi\-layered team of managers and data center technicians across three clusters, fostering a high\-performance, inclusive, and safety\-first culture
* Own workforce planning, staffing, and talent development including recruiting, onboarding, training, and performance management


 A day in the life
   

  

 As the Rack Lifecycle Cluster Manager for BOM, no two days are the same. You might start your morning reviewing a multi\-cluster capacity delivery dashboard, identifying a supply chain risk for an upcoming rack wave, and aligning with your managers on mitigation actions. By midday, you could be in a cross\-functional war room with Capacity Planning and Network Engineering to unblock a stalled rack installation, or reviewing decom flightpath trends ahead of a WBR. In the afternoon, you might be coaching a manager on stakeholder communication, reviewing a new SOP for Late Binding cabling standards, or presenting cluster performance metrics to senior AWS leadership. Your role is equal parts strategic and operational — you set the direction, remove the barriers, and develop the people who execute at the ground level.
   

  

**About The Team**
 About AWS
   

  

 Diverse Experiences
   

  

 Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
   

  

 Why AWS
   

  

 Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
   

  

 Work/Life Balance
   

  

 We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.
   

  

 Inclusive Team Culture
   

  

 AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.
   

  

 Mentorship and Career Growth
   

  

 We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* Bachelor's degree or above in Engineering (any domain), Operations, Business Administration, or a related field
* Experience working with multiple groups of stakeholders
* Speak, write, and read fluently in English, and have the ability to take direction in English
* 10\+ years of data center engineering or operations, or 5\+ years of relevant discipline work experience
* 5\+ years of people management, including 2\+ years as a manager of managers experience
* Experience leading the design, automation, deployment, and support of large\-scale infrastructure along with experience in project and program management


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience working in a data center environment (racking and stacking gear, cable management, hot/cold aisle containment)
* Experience leading cross\-functional projects and managing large, complicated programs
* Experience architecting/operating solutions built on AWS
* Knowledge of critical data center equipment
* Experience identifying and aggregating trends, past performance metrics, and insights into effective narratives, with recommendations, to external senior leaders
* 8\+ years of driving process improvements experience


 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. bd359893.title: Senior Program Manager, Audits and Insights Team
bd359893.description:
**Description**
 Amazon.com strives to be Earth's most customer\-centric company where people can find and discover virtually anything they want to buy online. By giving customers more of what they want \- low prices, vast selection, and convenience \- Amazon.com continues to grow and evolve as a world\-class e\-commerce platform.
   

  

 The AIT Program Manager is responsible for driving short term and long\-term strategic projects by closely coordinating with business leaders. The role will require quickly learning audit strategy to grasp the big picture in order to develop metrics, strategies and processes. You will be working with a number of stakeholders including Operations, Analytics, Policy, Technology teams to correlate business and operational outcomes, unlocking operational constraints and identifying opportunity to streamline the processes. This role requires high level strategic thinking, effective communication and deep\-dive analytical problem solving.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* Program \& Change Management:
* Lead and oversee complex change requests within audit programs, ensuring high\-quality implementation and meaningful outcomes
* Develop and execute strategic initiatives that drive continuous improvement in audit methodologies
* Design and implement scalable solutions for program optimization
* Establish and maintain program management best practices and standards
* Develop risk\-based prioritization models for audit programs considering business criticality and resource availability
* Establish metrics and KPIs to measure program success and ROI
* Spearhead AI and Automation Strategy for Audits:
* Design and implement workflow automation solutions to enhance program efficiency and effectiveness
* Develop a comprehensive AI \& Automation roadmap for audit processes
* Identify key areas for AI \& Automation integration (risk assessment, anomaly detection, etc.)
* Align AI \& Automation initiatives with overall audit objectives and compliance requirements
* Partner with policy program owners to prioritize AI projects
* Strategic Leadership and performance improvement:
* Partner with senior stakeholders to identify opportunities for performance improvement
* Evaluate and prioritize change requests based on effort, impact, and opportunity cost
* Drive data\-driven decision\-making through sophisticated analysis and insights generation
* Lead cross\-functional teams to execute high\-priority initiatives
* Business Performance \& Reporting:
* Develop and maintain comprehensive business performance reporting frameworks for executive leadership
* Co\-create and automate performance dashboards that highlight key metrics, trends, and areas of concern
* Drive data\-informed decision\-making through regular performance reviews and impact assessments
* Establish audit prioritization frameworks based on business impact, risk levels, and resource constraints
* Stakeholder Management:
* Build and maintain strong relationships with senior leadership and key stakeholders
* Influence and navigate complex organizational dynamics
* Present recommendations and insights to executive leadership
* Lead change management efforts across multiple stakeholder groups


**About The Team**
 Amazon.com strives to be Earth's most customer\-centric company where people can find and discover virtually anything they want to buy online. By giving customers more of what they want \- low prices, vast selection, and convenience \- Amazon.com continues to grow and evolve as a world\-class e\-commerce platform.
   

  

 The AIT Program Manager is responsible for driving short term and long\-term strategic projects by closely coordinating with business leaders. The role will require quickly learning audit strategy to grasp the big picture in order to develop metrics, strategies and processes. You will be working with a number of stakeholders including Operations, Analytics, Policy, Technology teams to correlate business and operational outcomes, unlocking operational constraints and identifying opportunity to streamline the processes. This role requires high level strategic thinking, effective communication and deep\-dive analytical problem solving.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 7\+ years of program or project management experience
* Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
* Experience owning program strategy, end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership
* Bachelor's degree


**Preferred Qualifications**

81b4818b.title: Senior Technical Program Manager
81b4818b.description:
**Summary**
 Guidewire provides outstanding software for the second\-largest financial services industry in the world: insurance. We deliver the core applications that Property and Casualty (P/C) insurers use to build their products, sell policies, settle claims, and bill their customers. We deliver SaaS solutions via Guidewire Cloud that enable our customers to rapidly innovate and drive measurable value. Our Application Platform helps internal developers and customers build innovative insurance applications with the aim to boost developer productivity, establish a vendor ecosystem and leverage the latest cloud technologies.
   

  

 As a Technical Program Manager, you will play a key role within Application Platform organization, driving projects internally (and with other teams). You will be responsible to drive and coordinate major platform and cloud service initiatives with the highest degree of complexity. Your impact will be felt across the organization. You will spend a significant proportion of your time developing the capabilities of the team as a whole.
   

  

**Job Description**
 What you’ll do
   

  

* Lead end‑to‑end planning and execution of complex, multi‑team technical programs across platform, cloud operations, product engineering, data, and security to support PDO’s goals in reliability, security, and gross margin improvement.
* Partner with engineering, product management, design, QA, business stakeholders, and infrastructure teams to define scope, problem statements, success metrics, and delivery milestones for initiatives such as GenAI “lifts,” cloud reliability improvements, and new platform capabilities.
* Develop, maintain, and continuously refine integrated program plans—including timelines, dependency maps, risk registers, and release plans—to ensure programs land on time, within scope, and with clearly defined business outcomes.
* Manage cross‑functional dependencies, proactively identify and remove blockers, and escalate risks with data‑driven options so teams can move quickly while maintaining quality, compliance, and customer trust.
* Facilitate agile ceremonies, technical deep dives, architecture and design discussions, and regular program syncs that align teams around priorities, trade‑offs, and delivery commitments across loosely coupled domains.
* Implement and evolve program governance, reporting cadences, and operational best practices that improve transparency, decision‑making, and execution efficiency across PDO and partner organizations.
* Define and own program‑level KPIs (e.g., reliability, incident metrics, development velocity, technical debt reduction, cloud and data platform adoption, infrastructure stability), track variances, and drive course‑corrections based on data and continuous feedback.
* Ensure technical readiness for production releases—including security posture, scalability, performance, disaster recovery dependencies, and operational health—aligned with Guidewire’s security and compliance standards.
* Collaborate with leadership to drive change management, refine processes, and align programs with Guidewire’s broader vision to embed AI and GenAI across products and operations, including initiatives like the Agentic AI Platform and cloud adoption accelerators.
* Influence platform and tooling direction by identifying opportunities to streamline developer workflows, improve operational efficiency, and better support AI, cloud, and data platform adoption across PDO and other teams.
* Manage relationships with vendors and third‑party service providers where applicable, including contract and SLA considerations, to ensure external dependencies support program outcomes and service reliability.
* Act as the “glue” that connects high‑velocity, distributed teams, ensuring alignment with Guidewire’s mission to transform how P\&C insurers operate through a secure, scalable, AI‑enabled cloud platform.
* At Guidewire, we foster a culture of curiosity, innovation, and responsible use of AI—empowering our teams to continuously leverage emerging technologies and data\-driven insights to enhance productivity and outcomes.


**Required Skills \& Experience**
* 12\+ years of total professional experience, including 5\+ years in technical program management driving complex, multi‑team software initiatives, preferably in cloud, infrastructure, or platform environments.
* Strong understanding of the software development lifecycle and agile or hybrid delivery models, with the ability to work effectively with engineering, product, operations, and security teams.
* Solid grasp of modern software architecture, cloud systems (e.g., distributed, multi‑tenant SaaS), and platform engineering concepts such as reliability, observability, and scalability.
* Proven track record of designing and running metrics‑driven programs: defining KPIs, building dashboards or reporting mechanisms, and using data to guide decisions, communicate impact, and improve program performance.
* Hands‑on experience with engineering planning and tracking tools (e.g., JIRA, Aha!, Confluence, Google Sheets) to manage backlogs, dependencies, and status reporting at scale.
* Exceptional stakeholder management and communication skills, including the ability to simplify complex technical topics for executives and non‑technical partners, and to drive alignment among diverse viewpoints.
* Proficiency with change management principles and techniques—able to guide teams through new processes, tools, and ways of working while minimizing disruption and resistance.
* Demonstrated ability to embrace AI and apply it to your current role as well as data\-driven insights to drive innovation, productivity, and continuous improvement.


**Preferred Skills \& Experience**
* Experience leading programs related to cloud operations, platform reliability, AI/ML or GenAI features, or large‑scale data platforms.
* Background in or exposure to P\&C insurance or other highly regulated industries, particularly in cloud or SaaS environments.
* Experience with vendor management, including understanding of SLAs, contract implications, and how external partners fit into critical program paths.
* Prior experience in organizations undergoing significant cloud, AI, or data platform transformation, especially where operational excellence and innovation must be balanced.
* BS/MS in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field—or equivalent demonstrated technical proficiency through experience.


 Your Impact
   

  

 We believe in clarity and setting you up for success. In your first months, you’ll immerse yourself in PDO’s FY26 VPMOM, understand our AI, cloud, and data platform priorities, and take ownership of one or more high‑impact programs—such as reliability and security improvements, GenAI‑enabled product capabilities, or cross‑region disaster recovery and readiness initiatives.
   

  

 You’ll establish program structures, clarify goals and KPIs, and build trusted relationships with engineering, product, security, operations, and go‑to‑market leaders. As your programs mature, you’ll be accountable for measurable outcomes like improved service reliability, faster time‑to‑market for AI‑infused features, increased adoption of cloud and data platform capabilities, and reduced operational toil. Your work will directly support Guidewire’s mission to combine digital, core, analytics, and AI into a cloud platform that helps P\&C insurers operate more efficiently and deliver better experiences to their customers worldwide.
   

  

**About Guidewire**
 Guidewire is the platform P\&C insurers trust to engage, innovate, and grow efficiently. We combine digital, core, analytics, and AI to deliver our platform as a cloud service. More than 540\+ insurers in 40 countries, from new ventures to the largest and most complex in the world, run on Guidewire.
   

  

 As a partner to our customers, we continually evolve to enable their success. We are proud of our unparalleled implementation track record with 1600\+ successful projects, supported by the largest R\&D team and partner ecosystem in the industry. Our Marketplace provides hundreds of applications that accelerate integration, localization, and innovation.
   

  

 For more information, please visit www.guidewire.com and follow us on Twitter: @Guidewire\_PandC.
   

  

 Guidewire Software, Inc. is proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We are committed to an inclusive workplace, and believe that a diversity of perspectives, abilities, and cultures is a key to our success. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship, marital status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability. All offers are contingent upon passing a criminal history and other background checks where it's applicable to the position.

c5d1a51d.title: Sr. Program Manager, Disaster Relief, Amazon Community Impact
c5d1a51d.description:
**Description**
 Join us at the forefront of Amazon Community Impact (ACI) initiatives to address societal problems. At Amazon, we're working to be the most customer\-centric company on earth. To get there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people who are passionate about making meaningful impact on communities while helping shape the future of community service. Through innovative programs and strategic partnerships, we create lasting positive change in the communities where we operate and advance Amazon's commitment to global stewardship and social responsibility.
   

  

 The ACI organization capitalizes on Amazon's scale and speed to build a more resilient and connected company. With over 1\.5 million Amazonians across 60\+ countries, we connect our people to community initiatives through volunteering to scale collective impact. Volunteering amplifies our Global Community Impact Giving priorities in disaster relief, food security, and education.
   

  

 Amazon's Disaster Relief team applies the same logistics expertise that delivers packages daily to get critical aid where it's needed within hours. We operate 15 disaster relief hubs globally, stocked with emergency supplies like blankets, medical devices, and tarps. Working alongside local organizations and first responders, we leverage Amazon's core capabilities in logistics, technology, and innovation to support communities in crisis. Since 2017, we've donated and delivered more than 26 million relief items across 200\+ disasters worldwide.
   

  

 The Disaster Relief team is looking for a passionate and innovative Sr. Program Manager to support Amazon's Disaster Relief Program.
   

  

 The ideal candidate will understand the complexities of global disaster response and how Amazon's unique capabilities ( product selection, logistics, warehousing, and technology) can be leveraged to support affected communities. To be successful in this role, you will need to monitor natural disasters year\-round and respond quickly when they occur, launching activations that include corporate product donations, disaster response technology services, and logistics support. You will operate and maintain a network of globally pre\-positioned relief supply hubs to enable faster response times, and create and scale playbooks that address the steps to support basic needs during disasters.
   

  

 The ideal candidate will also be comfortable building channels for customers and employees to participate in relief efforts, and will thrive in an environment that requires constant adaptation as situations evolve on the ground.
   

  

 The position can be based in Bangalore, IN.
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* Lead disaster relief operations and incident command for activations across the Asia Pacific (APAC) region
* Manage pre\-positioning hubs in the region, including inventory systems, vendor coordination, and logistics partnerships
* Define and iterate on regional in\-kind product donation strategy and delivery mechanisms
* Influence global hub strategy using regional data and operational experience
* Coordinate across procurement, transportation, and logistics partners to drive operational efficiencies
* Enhance playbooks and operational excellence standards across all regional offerings
* Manage stakeholder relationships across internal teams and external partners during activations


**About The Team**
 Diverse Experiences:
   

  

 ACI values diverse experiences. We encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
   

  

**Inclusive Team Culture**
 It’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (inclusive diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.
   

  

**Mentorship \& Career Growth**
 We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 5\+ years of working cross functionally with tech and non\-tech teams experience
* 5\+ years of program or project management experience
* 5\+ years of delivering cross functional projects experience
* Experience defining program requirements and using data and metrics to determine improvements


**Preferred Qualifications**
* 2\+ years of driving process improvements experience
* Master's degree, or MBA in business, operations, human resources, adult education, organizational development, instructional design or related field
* Experience in a high\-volume environment, such as a security operations center, event security, call center, or crisis management/emergency response center
* Experience working effectively across cross\-functional teams and partnering well with people at all levels within an organization
* \- Experience in retail, operations, supply chain, transportation, or warehousing/inventory management
* \- Experience developing and implementing operational excellence programs and training initiatives
* \- Experience writing playbooks, creating mechanisms, and delivering measurable results in ambiguous, agile environments
* \- Passion for disaster relief; direct disaster relief experience preferred


 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
   

  

  

**Company**

b432aded.title: Technical Program Manager (Data & ML Platform) (5 to 8 years)
b432aded.description:
**Summary**
 The Data \& ML Platform team sits within Product Development \& Operations (PDO) and powers Guidewire’s next\-generation cloud, analytics, and AI capabilities for P\&C insurers worldwide. We deliver secure, scalable data and ML services that enable product teams to ship intelligent features with confidence, reliability, and operational excellence. You’ll join a collaborative, globally distributed engineering and product community that values curiosity, continuous improvement, and pragmatic innovation.
   

  

 As a Technical Program Manager, you will drive complex, cross\-functional initiatives that unlock the full potential of Guidewire’s cloud\-based Data \& ML Platform—enabling AI\-infused products, high\-quality data pipelines, and scalable ML capabilities across our portfolio. You’ll partner closely with Engineering, Data Science, Product, SRE, Security, and Operations to plan, execute, and land programs that improve performance, reliability, and customer value. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in ambiguity, brings structure to fast\-paced environments, and is excited to help PDO deliver AI, cloud, and data platform innovation at scale for global P\&C insurers.
   

  

**Job Description**
**What you’ll do**
* Own end\-to\-end delivery of high\-impact programs on the Data \& ML Platform roadmap—from scope and planning through launch and post\-release outcomes—ensuring alignment to PDO’s priorities around AI, cloud, and data platform adoption.
* Partner with Engineering Pods, Data Scientists, Product Managers, Product Operations, SRE, and Security to design program plans, clarify requirements, manage trade\-offs, and keep teams focused on measurable outcomes like reliability, performance, and adoption.
* Drive a strong culture of data quality and observability across platform services and ML models, enabling trusted analytics, efficient operations, and reliable AI features for customers.
* Identify, manage, and communicate cross\-team dependencies and integrations across microservices, distributed systems, and data platforms, proactively mitigating risks and clearing delivery roadblocks.
* Implement and refine Agile/Scrum/Kanban practices across teams to improve predictability, transparency, and throughput, while promoting continuous improvement in execution, tooling, and ways of working.
* Build and maintain program plans and schedules that clearly highlight milestones, critical paths, risks, and decision points, providing concise, well\-structured updates and dashboards for technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* Facilitate effective program governance by running cross\-functional ceremonies, guiding teams on best practices, and ensuring accountability to PDO’s operational excellence and service reliability goals.
* At Guidewire, we foster a culture of curiosity, innovation, and responsible use of AI—empowering our teams to continuously leverage emerging technologies and data\-driven insights to enhance productivity and outcomes.


**Required**
**What you’ll bring**
* Demonstrated ability to embrace AI and apply it to your current role as well as data\-driven insights to drive innovation, productivity, and continuous improvement.
* 5\+ years of experience managing complex, cross\-functional software or cloud infrastructure programs in a dynamic environment, with a track record of delivering against clear outcomes.
* Strong technical fluency with cloud\-native architectures and concepts such as microservices, distributed systems, APIs, and event\-driven or data\-intensive platforms (experience with AWS and/or Big Data ecosystems is a plus).
* Proven experience working closely with engineering and product teams—translating strategy into executable roadmaps, resolving ambiguity, and managing risk and dependencies at scale.
* Excellent communication, collaboration, and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence across organizational levels and time zones through clear written, verbal, and visual communication.
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with comfort using metrics and dashboards to inform prioritization, trade\-offs, and program decisions.
* Experience with Agile methods and tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence, Aha or similar) to drive transparency, reporting, and continuous improvement in delivery.
* Ability to operate with resilience and composure in a fast\-paced environment, balancing short\-term execution needs with longer\-term platform and customer value.


**Preferred**
* Experience in data or ML platform environments, working closely with Data Science and Engineering teams to bring models and data products into production.
* Background in P\&C insurance, financial services, or other complex, highly regulated domains.
* Familiarity with SRE, security, and reliability concepts (e.g., SLIs/SLOs, incident management, security and compliance controls) in cloud environments.
* Experience using or piloting AI capabilities within project tracking, reporting, or productivity tools to improve forecasting, risk detection, and status communication.
* Professional program/project management certifications (e.g., PMP, PMI\-ACP, PgMP, SAFe) or equivalent experience leading large cross\-functional programs.


**Your Impact**
 We believe in clarity and setting you up for success. In your first six months, you will take ownership of one or more key Data \& ML Platform initiatives that are central to PDO’s FY26 priorities—such as driving adoption of AI\-powered capabilities, improving platform reliability, or scaling data infrastructure that supports new product experiences for global customers. You’ll partner with Engineering and Product leaders to define clear objectives, lock in cross\-team commitments, establish governance and communication rhythms, and build execution plans that track measurable outcomes like feature adoption, data quality, incident reduction, or time\-to\-delivery. As you build credibility, you’ll become a go\-to leader for orchestrating complex programs that help Guidewire transform how P\&C insurers operate—advancing our mission to combine digital, core, analytics, and AI as a cloud service so every customer can realize measurable value and efficiency from our platform.
   

  

**About Guidewire**
 Guidewire is the platform P\&C insurers trust to engage, innovate, and grow efficiently. We combine digital, core, analytics, and AI to deliver our platform as a cloud service. More than 540\+ insurers in 40 countries, from new ventures to the largest and most complex in the world, run on Guidewire.
   

  

 As a partner to our customers, we continually evolve to enable their success. We are proud of our unparalleled implementation track record with 1600\+ successful projects, supported by the largest R\&D team and partner ecosystem in the industry. Our Marketplace provides hundreds of applications that accelerate integration, localization, and innovation.
   

  

 For more information, please visit www.guidewire.com and follow us on Twitter: @Guidewire\_PandC.
   

  

 Guidewire Software, Inc. is proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We are committed to an inclusive workplace, and believe that a diversity of perspectives, abilities, and cultures is a key to our success. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship, marital status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability. All offers are contingent upon passing a criminal history and other background checks where it's applicable to the position.

0d3be9b7.title: Sr. Technical Program Manager, Amazon Pay | Credit & Lending
0d3be9b7.description:
**Description**
 Are you excited about building the future of digital lending at massive scale? Do you want to shape a greenfield lending platform that combines technology, compliance, risk, payments, accounting, and customer experience across emerging international markets?
   

  

 The Credit \& Lending team at Amazon Pay is building Amazon’s next\-generation lending platform to enable seamless credit experiences for millions of customers across Latin America. This is a high\-visibility, high\-ownership role at the intersection of fintech innovation, large\-scale distributed systems, regulatory compliance, and customer obsession.
   

  

 As a Senior TPM, you will lead complex cross\-functional programs spanning engineering, product, finance, tax, compliance, risk, legal, operations, and external financial partners. You will help define and drive the roadmap for Amazon’s in\-house lending capabilities across LATAM markets, enabling scalable and reusable technology foundations for onboarding, underwriting, servicing, repayments, accounting, and customer support experiences.
   

  

 This role requires someone who thrives in ambiguity, can operate at both strategic and execution depth, and is passionate about solving hard problems in a fast\-moving environment.
   

  

 What You’ll Do
   

  

 Lead end\-to\-end execution of in\-housed lending programs across LATAM markets.
   

  

 Drive alignment across engineering, product, finance, tax, compliance, legal, and external partners.
   

  

 Define program strategy, execution plans, milestones, risk mitigation mechanisms, and operational readiness.
   

  

 Partner with engineering leaders to build scalable, reusable, and resilient lending platform capabilities.
   

  

 Navigate highly ambiguous problem spaces involving regulatory and financial compliance requirements.
   

  

 Influence senior leadership through data\-driven decision making, trade\-off analysis, and clear communication.
   

  

 Drive launch readiness for critical customer\-facing experiences across onboarding, usage, servicing, and repayments.
   

  

 Establish operational excellence mechanisms including dependency management, issue resolution, escalation handling, and executive reporting.
   

  

 Work closely with external financial institutions, banking partners, and fintech providers to drive integrations and launch readiness.
   

  

 Help shape the long\-term vision for Amazon’s 1P Lending expansion across international markets.
   

  

 Why This Role Is Exciting
   

  

 Opportunity to build a greenfield lending platform at Amazon scale.
   

  

 Direct impact on the future of digital credit experiences for millions of customers.
   

  

 Solve highly complex technical, regulatory, accounting, and operational challenges.
   

  

 Exposure to senior leadership across multiple organizations.
   

  

 Ownership across both strategy and execution—not just program tracking.
   

  

 Work on cutting\-edge fintech systems involving real\-time underwriting, servicing, payments, accounting automation, tax compliance, and partner integrations.
   

  

 Drive cross\-region architectural leverage and reusable platform capabilities across markets.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 7\+ years of working directly with engineering teams experience
* 5\+ years of technical product or program management experience
* 3\+ years of software development experience
* 5\+ years of technical program management working directly with software engineering teams experience
* Experience managing programs across cross functional teams, building processes and coordinating release schedules
* Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition


**Preferred Qualifications**
* 5\+ years of project management disciplines including scope, schedule, budget, quality, along with risk and critical path management experience
* Experience managing projects across cross functional teams, building sustainable processes and coordinating release schedules
* Experience defining KPI's/SLA's used to drive multi\-million dollar businesses and reporting to senior leadership


 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

2f5c9221.title: Sr. Program Manager, RBS Returns Reduction
2f5c9221.description:
**Description**
 Amazon strives to be Earth’s most customer\-centric company, where customers can find and discover anything they want to buy online. Retail Business Services (RBS) accelerates Amazon's growth by improving customer and Selling Partner (SP) experiences, while optimizing costs across Stores WW. RBS operates at the intersection of retail, operations, technology, and finance, ensuring Amazon’s systems and processes scale reliably while maintaining a high\-quality customer and selling\-partner experience.
   

  

 Returns Reduction as part of Cost to Serve (CtS) BU is a specialized program within RBS focused on driving down overall Return Rate for both Retail and FBA channels. Returns Reduction Program focuses on Upstream defect elimination with focus on defect identification and defect elimination and partners with System Owners, Category Teams, FC ACES, Finance, CS, and abuse\-related programs to uncover true drivers and remove them systematically. The team builds mechanisms that strengthen upstream defect visibility, and enable consistent governance and contributing directly to improved Free Cash Flow and scalable business growth.
   

  

 This role will focus on identification and prioritization of high\-impact defect opportunities and will own and define the roadmap for elimination of the problem space end to end. They will do this by building trusted partnerships with key stakeholders across technology, finance, operations and earning alignment to pursue defect elimination opportunities and influencing senior leaders through compelling, data\-driven business cases. This role requires an individual who is comfortable in diving deep into complex data sets and presenting strategic recommendations to leadership up to three tiers above level. The candidate will bring strong business and supply chain acumen, a bias for action, and the strategic vision by directly contributing to improved cost to serve performance and driving scalable growth across Amazon Stores worldwide.
   

  

 Why You’ll Love This Role
   

  

* Customer Impact: You’ll lead critical projects that remove defects, improves customer experience and directly influence Amazon’s supply chain cost, driving measurable results.
* Strategic Influence: Collaborate across global teams, including technology, finance, operations to shape strategies that focuses on improving customer satisfaction.
* Data\-Driven Innovation: Leverage analytics to uncover insights and optimize supply chain performance.


 Key job responsibilities
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* Deliver Results, Dive Deep: You will be accountable for deep research from the ground up and from customer returns data to identify, execute and eliminate top defect opportunities.
* Ability to Influence: Develop business cases and present them to senior leaders, leveraging data to influence roadmaps and make tradeoffs.
* Earn Trust: Develop partnerships with key stakeholder groups and gain their alignment to pursue defect elimination opportunities; present to leaders of all levels to influence roadmap priorities.
* Financial Collaboration: Partner with finance teams to validate impact on returns, forecast savings and ensure alignment with team's financial goals. Make trade\-offs between short\-term cost reductions and long\-term strategic objectives to maximize financial impact.
* Managing stakeholder communication across multiple lines of business on various project milestones, process changes, escalations, etc.
* Provide ad\-hoc support and analysis to senior leaders on strategy documents and research requests.


 A day in the life
   

  

* Roadmap development: Use advanced analytics tools and anecdotes to identify high impact defect opportunities within Returns space; conduct root cause analysis to validate and develop those use cases, applying judgement to prioritize based on customer impact, value, credibility, availability of solutions.
* Cross\-Functional Leadership: Collaborate with cross\-functional teams across System owning orgs, finance, operations and supply chain to gain alignment on implementation of scalable solutions. Unblock teams to increase speed of delivery and ensure alignment on program goals.
* Senior Leadership Engagement: Communicate effectively with senior leaders, presenting data\-driven insights and recommendations to influence decision\-making. Trusted to present decisions to leaders up to three tiers above level, driving alignment across diverse areas.


**About The Team**
 The RBS is an integral part of Amazon online product lifecycle and buying operations. RBS Return reduction team functioning under CX\-Business unit. The primary responsibility of the RBS return reduction team is to reduce returns that result in negative consumer experiences. This includes identifying the causes of returns and collaborating with the appropriate teams to eliminate defects. Launch tech solutions to 'Find' and 'Fix' for non\-eliminated root causes, or Build scalable tech solutions for Selling Partners for self\-remediation, while refining policies in collaboration with relevant teams.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 5\+ years of working cross functionally with tech and non\-tech teams experience
* 5\+ years of program or project management experience
* 5\+ years of delivering cross functional projects experience
* Experience defining program requirements and using data and metrics to determine improvements


**Preferred Qualifications**
* 2\+ years of driving process improvements experience
* Master's degree, or MBA in business, operations, human resources, adult education, organizational development, instructional design or related field


 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

 \- ADCI \- BLR 14 SEZ \- F07

5652bb73.title: Solutions Architect, Conglomerates, AGS-APJ-India, Conglomerates SA Team
5652bb73.description:
**Description**
 AWS Global Sales (AGS) is responsible for driving revenue, adoption, and growth from the largest and fastest growing small\- and mid\-market accounts to enterprise\-level customers including public sector.
   

  

 Are you excited to work with the team that enables India’s conglomerates to build innovative services with AWS, scaling to serve billion\+ Indians? The Conglomerates team in AWS India is looking for a Solutions Architect who can work with our customers who are augmenting their existing business empires with new business models on the foundations of payment infrastructure like UPI, quick commerce, widespread 5G internet access, affordable smartphones, exploding digital content, identity infrastructure like Aadhaar and world class AI \& cloud infrastructure. These new businesses are rapidly and profoundly changing everyday life of billion\+ Indians through their platforms \& services for E\-commerce, Smart Factories, Connected Vehicles, Travel \& Hospitality, FinTech and many more.
   

  

 \#aws\-ags\-India
   

  

 Key job responsibilities
   

  

 Your broad responsibilities will include, owning the technical engagement and ultimate success around specific implementation projects, and developing deep expertise in the AWS technologies as well as broad know\-how around how applications and services are constructed using the AWS platform. An ideal candidate should have worked on solution design \& architecture of as part of a CoE, Cloud Practice team etc. Candidate must have have in\-depth experience one or more of following technologies \- Full stack application development/ DevOps / Databases / Networking / Security / Observability / Data Analytics \& AIML. Good understanding of patterns and frameworks and ability to identify trade\-offs is required. Candidate should have a flair for hands\-on coding to do Proof of Concepts and demonstrate technologies. Candidate should be good at identifying repeatable pattern and best practices, and able to learn new technologies and customer business domain.
   

  

 The ideal candidate will possess customer facing skills that will allow them to represent AWS India well within a customer’s or partner's environment and drive discussions with mid to senior personnel within the company, as well as a technical background that enables them to easily interact and give guidance to software developers and architects.
   

  

 Diverse Experiences
   

  

 Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
   

  

 Why AWS
   

  

 Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
   

  

 Work/Life Balance
   

  

 We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.
   

  

 Inclusive Team Culture
   

  

 Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (DEI) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.
   

  

 Mentorship and Career Growth
   

  

 We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional.
   

  

 A day in the life
   

  

 The selected candidate will work with subsidiairies / JV of large Indian conglomerates to map business requirements to technology. This will include but not limited to, understanding various design patterns \& discern between various data \& compute technologies. The candidate will also ensure right TCO while meeting the functional \& non\-functional requirements. The candidate will also guide on building DevSecOps, Observability \& Monitoring of Infrastructure \& Applications; conduct Well Architected Reviews; partner with AWS Domain \& Technology Specialists to implement innovative solutions; collaborate with the Service Teams to identify feature gaps \& engage with Technical communities and Customer C\-Suite on Technology strategy \& Best practices.
   

  

**About The Team**
 The Conglomerates team is one of the most diverse teams you would ever get in your career. Our team members have deep technology experience spanning areas of Analytics, IoT, AI/ML, GenAI, Observability, Resiliency \& Security. Top it up with years of domain expertise across Automotive, Manufacturing, e\-Commerce, Telco, Travel \& Hospitality \- this is the team where Tech meets Business for the largest movers \& shakers of India's push to $5T economy.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 10\+ years of IT development or implementation/consulting in the software or Internet industries experience
* 7\+ years of specific technology domain areas (e.g. software development, cloud computing, systems engineering, infrastructure, security, networking, data \& analytics) experience
* 3\+ years of design, implementation, or consulting in applications and infrastructures experience


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Experience with AWS technologies
* Experience working directly in customer implementations
* Experience migrating or transforming legacy customer solutions to the cloud
* Experience in technology/software sales, pre\-sales, or consulting


 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
   

  

  

**Company** 
 \- AWS India \- Karnataka
